symptomSummary: After a prompt revision accumulates ~30+ invocations, the evolve loop produces regressions by addressing stale flagged failures instead of recent ones.
rootCauseHypothesis: The evolution service fetches all historical flagged invocations for a revision without recency ordering or a sliding-window cap, so as invocation count grows older failures numerically dominate the sample passed to the LLM.
fixHypothesis: Add ORDER BY timestamp DESC + LIMIT N (or a date-range predicate) to the repository query that retrieves flagged invocations, so the evolve prompt is built from the most recent N failures rather than the full unbounded history.

primaryLocation.rationale: This file is solely responsible for assembling the LLM context used during evolution. The threshold-shaped failure (fine at low counts, breaks above ~30) is a classic sign that a growing list is passed in full rather than windowed.

candidateLocations.rationale: Recently modified file that almost certainly contains the SQL retrieving feedback rows. A missing ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT N would cause every fetch to return full unbounded history in insertion order, making old failures dominate as the table grows.
The evolve API route may be passing the raw unwindowed invocation list straight to the service without any limit/since parameter.
The UI component may be constructing the feedback payload and sending all loaded invocation rows without a recency cap.
Canonical evolve example; if the SDK-level helper aggregates feedback internally without a window, this illustrates the unguarded call site.
